patterns.summary: Next.js App Router pages must not render a raw <head> element. Use the Metadata API (export const metadata / generateMetadata) or, in the Pages Router, next/head's <Head> — a raw <head> is not hoisted or deduplicated and conflicts with Next.js head management.
bestPractices: Export a `metadata` object or `generateMetadata` from the route segment (App Router)
Use next/head's <Head> for per-page tags in the Pages Router
Never author a literal <head> element inside a component tree
antiPatterns: Rendering a raw <head> element inside a Next.js component
Duplicating <head> management instead of using the Metadata API

patterns.summary: Do not import server-only modules (database clients, secrets, server-only-marked packages) into Client Components. This is a cross-file import-boundary invariant — it is NOT expressible as a single-file forbid-selector and is recorded as a research-only finding (knowledge surfaced), NOT shipped as an executable rule in this slice.
bestPractices: Mark server-only modules with the `server-only` package so an accidental client import fails the build
Keep data-access code in Server Components or route handlers
Pass data to Client Components via props, not by importing server modules
antiPatterns: Importing a server-only module from a 'use client' file
Leaking secrets into a Client Component bundle
